title: Automatic Quantification of Cell Surface Coverage in Mono- and Co-Cultures Using Optical Microscopy and Small Neural Networks with Fiji
author: Kerkhoff, Yannic
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This protocol presents an automated method for analyzing cell surface coverage in mono- and co-cultures using phase contrast / fluorescence imaging and machine learning. The approach leverages Fiji (ImageJ) and the Trainable Weka Segmentation plugin to segment and quantify cell surface areas from input images. The protocol processes phase contrast and fluorescent images, applies Gaussian blur and thresholding techniques, and utilizes a pre-trained neural network classifier to distinguish between different cell types. The method calculates the surface coverage of total cells, fluorescent cells (HUVECs), and non-fluorescent cells (SMCs) in both mono- and co-cultures. The results are summarized in an output file, providing quantitative metrics such as total cell surface coverage, fluorescent surface coverage, and non-fluorescent surface coverage. This protocol offers a robust, automated solution for high-throughput cell culture analysis, enhancing reproducibility and efficiency in cell biology research.
